b: ^Sarah: Hi! You must be the IT contractor. I'm Sarah, the receptionist.
^Sarah: Let me get you checked in for the security audit.

first_checkin: ^Thanks. I'm here to audit your network security
^Just point me to IT and I'll get started

c-0: ^Sarah: Oh good! Kevin mentioned you'd be coming. He's been asking for a security review for months.
c-1: ^Sarah: Sure thing. Let me get you set up first.

receive_items: ^Sarah: Here's your visitor badge and a key for the main office area.
^Sarah: The office door is usually locked during audits—confidentiality protocols, you know.
^Sarah: Kevin should be in the IT room. It's through the main office, on the east side.
ask_it_location: ^Sarah: Go through the main office, then look for the door marked "IT" on the east wall.
^Sarah: The IT room has a keypad lock. Kevin's the one who knows the code.
^Sarah: Actually, I think there's a maintenance checklist somewhere in the main office with the codes. Kevin keeps forgetting them.
hub: ^I noticed the manager's office is vacant?

c-4: ^Sarah: Good luck with the audit! Let me know if you need anything.

ask_kevin: ^Sarah: Kevin's our IT manager. Really nice guy, kind of overworked.
^Sarah: He's been worried about security lately. Says someone's been accessing servers without authorization.

c-0: ^Sarah: I don't know. He mentioned it to management but nothing happened.
^Sarah: He seems stressed about it. Maybe you can help him figure it out.

ask_office_layout: ^Sarah: Main office is through that door—open plan with desks for the team.
^Sarah: • IT room on the east (where Kevin hangs out)
^Sarah: • Conference room also on the east
^Sarah: • Storage closet and break room on the west
^Sarah: • Private offices on the north—Derek, Maya, and the vacant manager's office

c-0: ^Sarah: Yeah, the senior staff each have their own space. Derek especially values his privacy.

ask_about_staff: ^Sarah: Well, there's Derek Lawson—Senior Marketing Manager. He's... intense.
^Sarah: Works late a lot. Like, really late. Sometimes I see his access logs from after midnight.

c-0: ^Sarah: Yeah. He says it's for client calls in different time zones, but...
^Sarah: I don't know. Something about him makes me uncomfortable.
c-1: ^Sarah: Maybe. Anyway, Maya Chen is nice. Content analyst. She's been here about a year.

ask_about_manager: ^Sarah: Oh, that was Patricia's office. She was our department manager.
^Sarah: She got fired about a month ago. Really sudden. "Performance issues" they said.

c-0: ^Sarah: Between us? Patricia was asking questions about Derek's projects.
^Sarah: Next thing you know, HR calls her in and she's gone.
^Sarah: Her briefcase is still in there. They escorted her out so fast she couldn't take everything.
c-1: ^Sarah: I guess. Her office has been empty since. It's kind of creepy.
